hero.title: Your Vote, Your Privacy, Your Voice
hero.subtitle: How PublicDigit protects your election integrity while keeping your vote completely anonymous
hero.promise: Vote with confidence. Your identity stays private. Your vote is anonymous. Election results are verifiable.
hero.ceo_tagline: Secure voting for organizations that value democracy
hero.ceo_description: Participate in elections knowing your vote is protected by enterprise-grade security and cryptography.

architecture.title: How We Protect Your Vote
architecture.subtitle: Five independent security layers protect every vote from entry to count
architecture.image_alt: Five-layer security architecture showing vote protection layers
architecture.fingerprint_subtitle: A four-layer technical approach that keeps you anonymous while proving you voted
architecture.fingerprint_diagram_alt: Technical diagram showing device data flowing through privacy layers
architecture.fingerprint_diagram_caption: This diagram shows your complete journey: collecting device data, encrypting it instantly, storing only the hash, and using it to verify votes without revealing identity.

fingerprint_layer1.title: Layer 1: Your Device
fingerprint_layer1.description: When you vote, your browser shares basic technical information needed for security verification:
fingerprint_layer1.note: ⚠️ Important: We collect this data temporarily, then immediately encrypt it before any storage.

fingerprint_layer2.title: Layer 2: One-Way Encryption
fingerprint_layer2.description: Before anything is stored, we instantly encrypt your device data using SHA256 with a secret salt from our server.
fingerprint_layer2.analogy: Think of it like grinding coffee beans:
fingerprint_layer2.analogy_forward: Coffee beans → Ground coffee ✅ (easy, one direction)
fingerprint_layer2.analogy_reverse: Ground coffee → Whole beans ❌ (impossible to reverse)
fingerprint_layer2.equation:
Your data + Secret salt → 🔒 Encrypted hash (a7f3d2c9...)
Encrypted hash → ??? ❌ (Cannot be reversed)

fingerprint_layer3.title: Layer 3: What We Store
fingerprint_layer3.description: We permanently store only encrypted data—never your raw information.

stored.fingerprint: Device Fingerprint Hash - The encrypted, one-way hash of your device (64 characters)
stored.metadata: Anonymized Metadata - Browser type (never version), device category (never model)

not_stored.mac_address: Device MAC address or serial numbers

fingerprint_layer4.title: Layer 4: Verification Without Revelation
fingerprint_layer4.description: The stored hash enables powerful security without compromising privacy:
fingerprint_layer4.same_device_desc: Verify it's you voting from your registered device
fingerprint_layer4.audit_desc: Cryptographic proof you voted (no identity revealed)
fingerprint_layer4.key_message: We can prove you voted AND that you only voted once... without ever knowing who you are.

device_data.user_agent: User Agent - Browser and operating system type
device_data.platform: Platform/OS - Windows, macOS, Linux, iOS, Android

layer1.title: Link Verification
layer1.description: Your voting code is validated and belongs to you
layer1.explanation: We check that your voting link is real, belongs to you, and is currently active.

layer2.title: Session Protection
layer2.description: Your voting session has expiration safeguards
layer2.explanation: Your session automatically expires after 24 hours for security. Votes are only accepted during official voting periods.

layer3.title: Organization Check
layer3.description: You can only vote in your organization's elections
layer3.explanation: The system ensures you're voting in the right election and belong to that organization.

layer4.title: Voting Rules
layer4.description: The system ensures one person, one vote
layer4.explanation: You can only vote once, with valid codes, for real candidates in the election.

layer5.title: Anonymous Storage
layer5.description: Your vote is stored with zero connection to your identity
layer5.explanation: We literally cannot see how you voted. Your selections are stored anonymously but remain verifiable.

summary: Five security layers mean your vote is protected at every step. Even if one layer were compromised, the other four still protect you.

anonymity.title: Complete Anonymity
anonymity.description: Your vote is stored with NO link to your identity. We literally cannot know how you voted - by design.

verification.title: Device Fingerprinting
verification.description: We store a secure, one-way hash of your device info - enough to detect fraud, impossible to identify you.

isolation.title: Receipt Verification
isolation.description: After voting, you get a receipt to verify your vote was counted - without revealing how you voted.

question1.question: Can PublicDigit see how I voted?
question1.answer: No. By design, we store NO link between your identity and your vote. Your vote is completely anonymous - even we cannot see it.

question2.question: What device information do you collect?
question2.answer: We collect basic browser/device information (IP, user agent, screen resolution) but we immediately hash it with a secret salt. Only the irreversible hash is stored - the original data is discarded. This lets us detect fraud without identifying you.

question3.question: Can I verify my vote was counted?
question3.answer: Yes! After voting, you receive a unique receipt string. Visit publicdigit.com/verify and enter it to confirm your vote was counted - without revealing how you voted.

question4.question: What if someone claims I didn't vote?
question4.answer: Election officials can use your IP + identity to generate a participation proof. This proves you voted without revealing your choice. It's like proving you were at the polling station without showing your ballot.

question5.question: Can my device fingerprint be traced back to me?
question5.answer: No. We use a one-way cryptographic hash with a secret salt. This is mathematically irreversible - it's like trying to un-bake a cake to find the recipe. Your privacy is absolute.

footer: Your Vote, Your Privacy, Your Voice
journey_subtitle: Five security checkpoints protect your vote
journey_image_alt: Voting journey showing five security verification points

question1.question: How is my vote kept anonymous?
question1.answer: Your vote is stored without any connection to your identity. We don't store voter IDs with votes - it's mathematically impossible to link your identity to your choices.

question2.question: Can I vote twice with the same code?
question2.answer: No. Each voting code can only be used once. After you vote, the code is permanently marked as used.

question3.question: Can I see the election results?
question3.answer: Yes. After voting closes, results are published. You can verify your vote was counted using the receipt you received after voting.

question4.question: What if I lose my receipt?
question4.answer: Contact your election administrator. They can provide a new participation verification without revealing how you voted.

stored.hashed_fingerprint: Hashed device fingerprint (one-way, can't be reversed)
stored.country_code: Country code only (from IP, not exact location)

not_stored.name: Your name
not_stored.user_id: Your user ID (votes have NO user link)

receipt.title: Receipt Verification
receipt.description: After voting, you receive a unique receipt string. Use it to verify your vote was counted - without revealing who you voted for.

auditor.title: Participation Proof
auditor.description: If someone claims they didn't vote, administrators can prove participation using IP + identity hash - without revealing the vote choice.

fingerprint.title: Device Security
fingerprint.why_title: Why we protect your device info:
fingerprint.why_intro: To prevent voting fraud while protecting your privacy. Device protection helps us detect:

reasons.ballot_stuffing: Multiple votes from the same device - Stops ballot stuffing
reasons.bots: Automated voting bots - Ensures real human voters
reasons.patterns: Suspicious voting patterns - Protects election integrity

fingerprint.how_note: The hash is mathematically impossible to reverse - we can never recover your actual device information.
